level_signals: 5+ years of experience building enterprise products
Title is 'Staff Product Manager' implying senior IC
Expected to 'grow a team that embodies engineering excellence'
Compensation range $220K-$260K base consistent with senior/staff IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience building enterprise products in a B2B/commercial environment
Experience working on Integrations and partnering with cross functional and Partnerships teams
Experience with or knowledge of AI and LLMs
Ability to partner with engineers on technical architecture, with enough depth to challenge assumptions, propose solutions, and influence design.
Ability to navigate extreme ambiguity to deliver outcomes
A product mindset for balancing user needs, domain complexity, and system constraints to propose high-leverage improvements.

scope.surface: Enterprise integrations with strategic partners/vendors, MCP-based search, deep research, legal context ingestion into Harvey platform
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / enterprise, legal and professional services AI platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Breadth and depth of integrations shipped
Scalability and reliability of platform integration layer
core_responsibilities: Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and strategy to execution and launch
Collaborate closely with engineering and partnerships team to identify and execute on high-impact integrations
Work closely with engineers to build, platformize and scale integrations, working cross functionally with adjacent teams
Expand the breadth and depth of integrations into the Harvey platform

Collaborate cross-functionally with Product Engineering, Applied AI, Research, and Platform teams to deliver high-quality production systems that support reasoning, summarization, and legal research workflows

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($220K-$260K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area (integrations, MCP, deep research)
Strong product-market fit signaled (1000+ customers, 60+ countries)
Cutting-edge applied AI work (MCP, frontier agentic AI)
red_flags: Hybrid in-person model (3 days/week) in San Francisco only — eliminates remote candidates
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization support
Equity not mentioned in compensation section despite being a high-growth startup
Role implies people management ('grow a team') but manages_people is ambiguous in JD; may be future expectation

seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'grow a team that embodies engineering excellence')
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer base)

ambiguities: manages_people: JD says 'grow a team' which implies future team building/management, but no explicit statement of current direct reports — treated as true given the language
